trigger.reason: Google's AI agent ecosystem announcement at I/O is a major capability demo from a top-tier lab targeting broad consumer adoption, likely to trigger sympathy-fade selling across low-code dev tools, customer-service software, and business automation SaaS.

primary_event.headline: Google pitches AI agent ecosystem to consumers at I/O
primary_event.summary: Google announced a new AI agent ecosystem at its I/O developer conference, positioning AI agents as a major consumer-facing capability for automating web-based tasks. The announcement represents a significant expansion of AI's role in consumer productivity workflows.

secondary_events.headline: Anthropic says it's about to have its first profitable quarter
Google reshaping search with AI overview feature expansion

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Google DeepMind's AI agent ecosystem announcement at I/O — capability demo targeting consumer web-task automation, hypothesized to pressure low-code, customer-service software, business automation SaaS.
discovery.run_summary: Today's sympathy-fade pattern is thin: of three candidates, two (AEIS, WK) actually moved UP on the trigger, undercutting any panic-fade buy thesis. Only BLKB shows a clean negative sympathy move, and its filings reveal genuine — though not direct — AI exposure with explicit agentic-AI language. Net: one OVERDONE candidate (BLKB on partial threat/overdone reaction), two skips because the price didn't actually fade.

discoveries.name: Blackbaud, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K explicitly discusses agentic AI risks BUT frames them as Blackbaud's OWN deployment of agentic features in its solutions, not as a competitive displacement vector. The competition risk language is generic ('introduction of solutions encompassing new technologies, such as AI, can render existing solutions obsolete') without naming consumer agents as a specific threat to nonprofit/donor workflows. Core moats — donor history depth, tax-exempt accounting, payment integration — are not flagged as AI-vulnerable.
discoveries.thesis: Google's consumer-facing web-task agent has essentially zero plausible overlap with Blackbaud's vertical: 20+ years of donor history per customer, tax-exempt accounting workflows, Blackbaud Integrated Payments, three-year subscription terms with ~40% of contracts renewing in 2026. Retail is selling 'SaaS exposed to AI' generically; the curation hypothesis (vertical workflow moat) holds up in the filings. The sharp single-day drop on a sub-average volume day looks more like sentiment than fundamentals — no 8-K, no concurrent catalyst.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable next-quarter renewal metrics; bounce-back within 3-5 sessions as the I/O news cycle fades; management commentary on agentic AI as a feature, not a threat
discoveries.what_kills: A nonprofit-specific AI fundraising tool announcement; a customer logo loss disclosed in next 10-Q; an 8-K with material guidance cut; Blackbaud's own AI product struggling and being cited as a competitive failure (the 10-K warns market perception of AI strategy can drive stock volatility independent of fundamentals — that risk is real here)
discoveries.what_to_learn: Vertical-SaaS names with deep proprietary data moats (donor history, regulatory tagging) sell off on every horizontal-AI demo regardless of business-model overlap. Track BLKB through multiple Google/OpenAI consumer-AI cycles to measure the fade decay.
discoveries.catalyst: Google pitches AI agent ecosystem to consumers at I/O
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: BLKB is a SaaS workflow tool — falls squarely in the trigger's 'business process automation / workflow management' blast radius even though its vertical (nonprofit fundraising) is not what consumer agents target.
research_pointers: Pull last 4 quarters of gross retention rate — has it dipped below the stated 94%+ historical?
Check whether any nonprofit-focused AI fundraising startups (e.g., Givebutter, Bonterra) have raised meaningful rounds or shipped agentic products
Compare BLKB drawdown to the broader vertical-SaaS basket on prior Google/OpenAI demo days
Review Blackbaud's own AI product roadmap announcements — are they positioned as agent-augmented or agent-replaced?

skipped.reason: Stock moved UP today — not a sympathy-fade candidate. Curation correctly flags it as a picks-and-shovels AI capex beneficiary; the price action confirms the market sees it that way.
Stock moved UP today and is up sharply on a 5-day basis — no panic-fade to fade. The compliance/XBRL moat thesis may be right, but there's no overdone selling to buy.
